title: Stone pattern marbling
sources: Josef Halfer, The Progress of the Marbling Art (1893, public domain) — the stone pattern as the foundational marbling form
notes: The stone pattern is the first step in almost all carrageenan-bath marbling: colours dropped with a dropper or pipette onto the size, each colour pushing the previous outward, creating concentric rings with ragged edges — resembling a cross-section of stone. No raking or combing. A finished stone pattern is a valid result in itself; it is also the raw starting material for combed patterns.
